mechanism_narrative: The Federal Reserve has raised the federal funds rate to 0.79% as of September 2022, signaling an active monetary tightening cycle. Continued elevated FEDFUNDS above 0.5% sustains upward pressure on long-term Treasury yields as markets price in persistent inflation-fighting policy. Higher short rates compress the term premium and push 10-year yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 0.5%, or Rank IC of FEDFUNDS-to-yield relationship falls below 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 243.752 (September 2022) remains elevated above pre-pandemic trend. If CPI continues to rise month-over-month or fails to decelerate below 0.3% monthly, the Fed will maintain or accelerate rate hikes, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as markets extend the tightening cycle duration.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly change > 0.3% or year-over-year inflation > 8%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite CPI trigger activation, or correlation between CPI surprises and 20-day yield changes drops below 0.10 over any 6-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 4.5% (September 2022) remains below the Fed's estimated natural rate, indicating a tight labor market. If UNRATE remains below 4.8% and PAYEMS growth stays positive, the Fed will interpret this as persistent inflation pressure and maintain hawkish policy, supporting higher 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% and PAYEMS at 145858.0 in September 2022
regime_hint: tight labor market with hawkish Fed response
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines when both UNRATE < 4.8% and PAYEMS growth positive, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.08 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 16813.3 billion (Q2 2022) shows the economy remains in expansion, but retail sales (RSAFS) at 470844.0 million (September 2022) signal consumer demand resilience. If demand remains strong, the Fed will maintain restrictive policy longer, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the terminal rate expectation extends.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change > 0.2% AND GDPC1 growth > 0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 470844.0 and GDPC1 at 16813.3 in Q2-Q3 2022
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite both RSAFS and GDPC1 growth signals active, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.07 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 103.7332 (August 2022) remains above pre-pandemic levels but growth has slowed. If INDPRO decelerates or contracts month-over-month, it signals weakening economic activity that may force the Fed to pause rate hikes sooner, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as terminal rate expectations decline.
regime_hint: supply-side slowdown with Fed policy reversal risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when INDPRO declines, or INDPRO-to-yield correlation remains below 0.08 in absolute value over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1288.0 thousand (August 2022) reflect weakening residential demand as mortgage rates rise. If HOUST falls below 1200 thousand, it signals demand destruction from Fed tightening, reducing inflation pressure and allowing the Fed to signal a pivot toward rate cuts, pushing 10-year Treasury yields lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when HOUST falls below 1200 thousand, or HOUST-to-yield correlation remains above -0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% (a level not yet reached as of October 2022 but anticipated in forward guidance), the Fed enters a restrictive regime that historically precedes economic slowdown. This triggers a regime shift where 10-year Treasury yields fall as growth expectations decline and the Fed begins to signal eventual rate cuts.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.79% in September 2022 with forward guidance suggesting further increases
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy regime with growth recession risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when trigger activates, indicating regime shift hypothesis is false.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that forces the Fed to abandon tightening and pivot to easing. This regime shift causes 10-year Treasury yields to fall sharply as terminal rate expectations collapse and growth recession fears dominate.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month AND PAYEMS monthly change < -50 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% and PAYEMS at 145858.0 in September 2022
regime_hint: labor market shock with Fed pivot to easing
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when both UNRATE spike and PAYEMS decline trigger simultaneously, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.10 over any 12-month rolling window.
